vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on passwd File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/passwd, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/passwd

triage_decision.reason: Changing /etc/passwd permissions to 0644 is a standard STIG remediation that does not affect system boot, networking, or critical services. The fix is a single chmod command, low risk of lockout, and aligns with policy for safe file permission fixes.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; authn/authz impact (passwd file used for authentication); potential mis‑set permissions if command typed incorrectly
